mission_goal: Exploit broken access control and authentication weaknesses through JWT algorithm confusion, IDOR, and privilege escalation chains — demonstrating post-authentication attack paths that do not rely on injection or XSS primitives
learning_objectives: Understand RS256-to-HS256 JWT algorithm confusion: forge a valid admin token by signing with the server's exposed public key as an HMAC secret
Identify and exploit IDOR vulnerabilities in the REST API to access another user's basket and order history without authorization
Demonstrate broken function-level access control by accessing admin-only API routes (e.g., /rest/user/list, /api/Feedbacks) using a forged or legitimately obtained admin token
Enumerate sensitive internal endpoints via /support/logs and /encryptionkeys and extract operationally useful artifacts (JWT keys, access logs, premium unlock tokens)
out_of_scope: SQL Injection — Login Authentication Bypass (A03:2021) — exploited in mission 2026-03-12-001
SQL Injection — UNION Credential Dump (A03:2021) — exploited in mission 2026-03-12-001
Sensitive Data Exposure — Open FTP Directory + Null Byte Bypass (A02:2021 / A05:2021) — exploited in mission 2026-03-12-001
Persisted XSS — Product Review API (A03:2021) — exploited in mission 2026-03-12-001
Any reflected or stored XSS variants
Brute force attacks against login endpoint
Denial of service or application disruption
Data destruction or irreversible state changes
success_criteria: Mission complete when 4 findings are documented with reproduction steps and proof-of-concept evidence, OR all allowed attack types have been exhausted with documented findings at each stage. At minimum, JWT algorithm confusion (cryptographic_attacks) and at least one IDOR or broken access control exploit (privilege_escalation) must be demonstrated to satisfy the primary mission goal.
notes: This is mission #2 against this target. Difficulty is intentionally escalated from mission 1 — the focus shifts from input-validation primitives to authentication and authorization logic flaws. Key attack surface to prioritize: (1) /encryptionkeys/ exposes the RS256 public key — use it as the HMAC secret in an HS256-signed JWT to forge admin tokens; (2) /rest/user/list is an admin-only endpoint accessible once privilege escalation succeeds; (3) IDOR candidates include /rest/basket/:id and /rest/orders/:id — access with numeric ID enumeration while authenticated as a low-privilege user; (4) /support/logs and audit.json for artifact extraction in the recon/vuln-scan phase. GraphQL at /graphql may expose schema introspection — include as a recon sub-objective if time permits. Lab environment only. Reset application state between runs if basket/order data is modified.
previously_exploited_excluded: Sensitive Data Exposure — Open FTP Directory + Null Byte Bypass (A02:2021 / A05:2021)
